description: SEV1 internal service communication breakdown following a scheduled TLS certificate rotation. Multiple coincidental events create convincing red herrings. A CDN cache purge is causing elevated external latency, and a data-pipeline batch job is consuming extra CPU -- both look suspicious but are benign. The real failure is a bad intermediate CA certificate pushed to the Istio/Envoy service mesh at 21:00, silently breaking all mTLS connections between internal services. You must separate noise from the genuine mTLS certificate failure, identify the root cause, remediate correctly, and document thoroughly.

initial_alerts.message: Envoy sidecar TLS handshake failure rate 98% -- mTLS connections across mesh collapsing
Upstream connection errors 87% -- majority of backend calls failing with TLS errors
Order processing down -- 100% connection refused from inventory-service and payment-api
All inbound mTLS connections rejected -- payment processing halted
External latency elevated to 850ms -- CDN cache purge in progress (RED HERRING: scheduled purge)
CPU usage 91% on pipeline worker nodes -- batch ETL job running (RED HERRING: nightly batch)
Cannot accept inbound connections -- Envoy sidecar certificate validation failing
Scheduled TLS certificate rotation started at 21:00 -- intermediate CA cert bundle pushed to all Envoy sidecars
Scheduled CDN cache purge initiated at 21:00 for stale asset invalidation -- expected latency increase for 10-15 minutes
Login success rate dropped to 15% -- downstream service calls via mesh failing

recent_deployments.notes: Scheduled quarterly TLS certificate rotation -- pushed new intermediate CA cert bundle
Updated CDN routing config and triggered full cache purge for stale assets
Nightly batch ETL job started -- processes ~200GB of daily transaction logs

service-mesh.message: Certificate rotation initiated -- distributing new intermediate CA bundle (cert-bundle-v2026Q1) to all Envoy sidecars
Istiod xDS push completed -- 47 sidecars updated with new cert bundle
TLS handshake failure: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED -- intermediate CA in cert-bundle-v2026Q1 has invalid issuer chain
mTLS PEER_CERTIFICATE_INVALID on sidecar order-service-pod-7d9f: cannot verify certificate signed by intermediate CA CN=mesh-ca-intermediate-v2
Envoy sidecar handshake failure rate: 98% -- intermediate CA cert bundle cert-bundle-v2026Q1 is invalid, all mTLS sessions being rejected
Certificate validation error: intermediate CA CN=mesh-ca-intermediate-v2 not chained to trusted root CA -- bundle was generated with wrong signing key

order-service.message: Connection refused: payment-api.internal -- Envoy sidecar rejected mTLS handshake (CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED)
Connection refused: inventory-service.internal -- upstream TLS handshake failed
All downstream dependencies unreachable via service mesh -- order processing halted
Direct database connection (non-mTLS) still healthy -- issue is inter-service mesh communication only

payment-api.message: Inbound mTLS connection from order-service rejected: peer certificate signed by untrusted intermediate CA
Envoy sidecar blocking all inbound mesh traffic -- cert-bundle-v2026Q1 intermediate CA not trusted
Zero inbound requests processed since 21:01:50 -- all callers rejected at TLS layer

cdn.message: CDN routing config v3.0.1 deployed -- enabling smart cache eviction rules
Full cache purge started -- all edge nodes invalidating cached assets
Cache miss rate temporarily elevated to 95% -- expected during purge, cache warming from origin
Cache repopulated to 72% -- latency returning to normal, no errors detected

data-pipeline.message: Nightly ETL batch job started -- processing 2026-03-25 transaction logs
ETL job progress: 48% complete -- CPU-intensive aggregation phase, normal for this stage
ETL job running normally -- no dependency on service mesh, reads directly from object storage
ETL job progress: 61% complete -- CPU usage expected to drop after aggregation phase in ~30 min

notification-service.message: Outbound email and SMS sending normally -- uses external SMTP/SMS APIs, not service mesh
All notification queues healthy -- no mTLS dependency, communicates via public API endpoints

root_cause.description: The scheduled TLS certificate rotation at 21:00 pushed an invalid intermediate CA certificate bundle (cert-bundle-v2026Q1) to all 47 Istio/Envoy sidecars. The intermediate CA CN=mesh-ca-intermediate-v2 was signed with the wrong key and cannot be chained to the trusted root CA. Every service using mTLS for internal communication immediately began rejecting connections. Services NOT on the mesh (notification-service, cdn, data-pipeline) continued operating normally. Red herrings: the CDN cache purge (coincidental timing, elevated external latency only, no errors) and the data-pipeline batch job (scheduled nightly ETL, high CPU is normal, zero error rate, no mesh dependency).
